title: ESG Data Analyst
description: **Job Description** **Job Title:** ESG Data Analyst **Function: Sustainability** **Reports to: Head of ESG reporting** **Location: Dublin** **12 month fixed term contract.** \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Ornua is a leading dairy co\-operative which sells premium dairy products globally on behalf of its Member Co\-operatives, Ireland’s dairy processors and, in turn, Irish dairy farmers. Ornua has revenues of €3\.4 billion and is supported by a global team of approximately 2,800 employees. The Group operates as a commercial organisation across 10 business units, including 12 production facilities located in Europe, North America, the Middle East and Africa. The commercial organisation is responsible for the marketing and sales of Ornua’s consumer brands including Ireland’s most successful food export: Kerrygold. Consumer markets are served by production facilities in Ireland, Germany and the UK and by in\-market Sales \& Marketing Teams in Asia, Germany, Ireland, MEA, Poland, Spain, rest of Europe and North and Latin America. It also manages the procurement of Irish and non\-Irish dairy products, the sale of dairy ingredients to food manufacturing and foodservice customers globally, and the implementation of de\-risking and trading strategies to manage market volatility. These activities are supported by production facilities and in\-market teams in Europe, North America, the Middle East and Africa. **The Role** An exciting opportunity has arisen in Ornua’s sustainability team for a Sustainability Data Analyst. The role will report to the Head of ESG Reporting, working closely with Ornua’s Group sustainability specialists, Group ESG Program team, operational sustainability leads and business units across Ornua’s global operations and offices. The ESG Data Analyst will strengthen Ornua’s sustainability reporting capability by partnering closely with internal and external stakeholders to understand data needs, enhance data integrity, and deliver decision‑ready ESG insights. The role ensures that high‑quality sustainability data flows reliably through Ornua’s systems, supports regulatory compliance, enables robust customer reporting, and informs strategic decision‑making at management, Executive, and Board level. **Key Areas of Responsibility** * Manage ESG data systems (e.g., Watershed, ERP integrations) and maintain strong governance, validation, and data quality controls. * Build and automate data pipelines and workflows to streamline and scale sustainability reporting across the Group. * Integrate data from multiple internal and external sources into a consistent, audit‑ready ESG data environment (Watershed). * Analyse ESG datasets to identify performance trends, risks, opportunities, and insights for business functions. * Develop and maintain dashboards, scorecards, reports, and visual insights for management, senior leadership, and the Board. * Translate complex data analytics into clear, actionable recommendations tailored to non‑technical audiences. * Collaborate cross‑functionally to enhance data processes, improve reporting capability, and build ESG literacy across the business. **Key Skills / Attributes** * Degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Engineering, Statistics, Computer Science or a related technical field. * Strong proficiency in SQL, Python, and data visualisation tools (e.g., Power BI). * Experience managing large datasets, developing data models, and building automated workflows. * Understanding of ERP systems and experience integrating complex operational data sources. * Knowledge on data extraction and transformation, data quality management, and system integrations. * Strong communication skills with the ability to convey technical insights to diverse stakeholders. * Proven ability to work independently, manage competing priorities, and deliver to tight deadlines. * Ability to initiate projects and drive them to completion under guidance. * Collaborative mindset aligned with Ornua’s values and a commitment to high‑integrity ESG reporting. **Ornua Core Competencies** * Customer Focus: Advocates for the customer, balances customer and business needs, and spots opportunities for growth. * Forward\-Looking: Helps others to accept change, implements change, and looks ahead to anticipate change. * Curious: Open to asking questions and willing to learn and continuously improve. * Working Together: Encourages co\-operation in own team and supports cross\-functional working. **Closing date: 22 May 2026**

title: Early Career Analyst Auto Claims Ireland
description: Early Career Analyst \- Auto Claims Ireland Who are we American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance in approximately 70 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets and manage risks. We’re also committed to making a positive difference for our colleagues and in the communities where we work and live. We encourage colleagues to give back to the causes they care most about, supporting these efforts through our Volunteer Time Off and Matching Grants Programs. Get to know the business General Insurance is a leading provider of insurance products and services for commercial and personal insurance customers. It includes one of the world’s most far\-reaching property casualty networks. General Insurance offers a broad range of products to customers through a diversified, multichannel distribution network. *At AIG, we value in\-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike.* Claims Division Our Claims Adjusters handle portfolios of complex claims from coverage enquiry through legal liability assessment (where relevant) and quantum analysis, to timely and accurate resolution. Our adjusters ensure mitigation of indemnity and expense exposure while communicating developments and outcomes as necessary to all key internal and external stakeholders, while providing a high standard of customer service and adherence to legal and regulatory requirements. **About The Role** At AIG, we are constantly working to ensure that all employees have the training and skills they need to have a rewarding and successful career. Designed with that commitment at its core, our Early Career program provides a platform for participants to launch or accelerate their careers. Through a range of approaches including innovative experiential learning and technical training, performance assessments, formalized mentoring, and exposure to senior leaders, our Early Career program offers unparalleled development opportunities for our diverse early career talent. Whether you’re a recent university graduate, someone with less than two years of work experience, or a professional seeking a new career path, the program is designed to evolve with the rapid pace of technological advancements and changing employee preferences to ensure that today’s participants become leaders capable of conquering tomorrow’s challenges. **You Will Be** * Contributing to a positive culture of teamwork, flexibility, learning and development. * Managing workloads to provide good service in a busy environment. * Driving performance with ability to work closely with leaders(s) and team(s). * Supporting our strategy and values within the team. * Finding solutions to difficult and challenging situations. * Ability to review our systems and processes, finding better more effective. ways.to do business, while keeping the customer focus. * Adhering to all service and reporting timelines agreed for our business. * Handling claims including appointing investigators and expert witnesses. * Working closely with brokers and AIG policyholders on the resolution of claims and provide a high level of customer service, including client review meetings. * Reserving accurately in line with AIG’s claims reserving philosophy. * Communicating with internal AIG stakeholders where required. * Timely, accurate and customer focused claim resolution, minimizing indemnity exposure and mitigating vendor and legal expense. **What We’re Looking For** AIG seeks candidates who have excelled in previous work experience, demonstrated outstanding academic and extracurricular achievement, possess strong analytical, quantitative, and interpersonal skills, and are enthusiastic about and committed to the insurance industry. The ideal candidates will be bold thinkers with an entrepreneurial bent and will possess a powerful blend of IQ (technical skills, consistent learning, statistical\&financialacumen)andEQ(adaptivecommunication,empathy, listening skills, challenging with humility). We also look for a diverse background of experience, culture, and thought. Successful candidates typically have a global perspective and a record of successful teamwork. **How To Apply** In order to be considered, an online application including CV is required. **Locations** AIG Claims Analyst positions are available in Dublin. **Qualifications Required** Minimum 2\.1 degree classification Pursuant of a bachelor’s or master’s degree, graduating in 2026 **A Look At Our Benefits** At AIG, we have a 100\-year legacy of working to make the world a better place. And that begins with our employees. We’re proud to offer a range of employee benefits and resources that help you protect what matters most – your health care, financial protection, and wellbeing. We provide a variety of leaves for personal, health, and family needs. For example, the “Giving Back” program allows you to take up to 16 hours a year to volunteer in your community. We also believe in fostering our employees’ development and offer a range of learning opportunities for employees to hone their professional skills to position themselves for the next steps of their careers. AIG also has a tuition reimbursement program for eligible At AIG, we value in\-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. **Enjoy Benefits That Take Care Of What Matters** At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. **Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world** American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far\-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever\-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. **Welcome to a culture of inclusion** We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. *AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories.* AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com. **Functional Area** CL \- Claims AIG Europe S.A. (Ireland branch)

title: Electrical Quantity Survayor
description: **Electrical Quantity Surveyor** If you’re an Electrical Quantity Surveyor who enjoys being involved from day one and still standing at the finish line, this role will suit you. You’ll take ownership of projects from tender through to final account, and you’ll have the commercial influence to actually make a dent in profitability — not just report on it afterwards. This isn’t a role where you sit in the background issuing spreadsheets into the void. You’ll be partnering closely with Project Managers, Engineers, subcontractors and clients, making sure the commercial heartbeat of each project stays healthy and predictable (or at least explainable). **What You’ll Be Doing (the Short Version)** * Building and managing BOQs, budgets, reports and profit plans without needing someone hovering over you. * Running interim commercial meetings with clients and internal teams — and being the person who knows exactly where the project stands commercially. * Managing subcontractor accounts, valuations and final accounts, ensuring payments follow agreed terms and retentions don’t fall through the cracks. * Issuing accurate monthly applications, including remeasures, dayworks, changes and any contract claims worth fighting for. * Spotting commercial risks early, raising them quickly and helping to keep surprises to a minimum. * Working closely with Planning Engineers to keep progress reporting aligned with reality rather than optimism. * Costing and agreeing daily work changes, including remeasurement of subcontractor work and site variations. * Keeping meticulous records — because commercial claims don’t win themselves. * Negotiating with clients or their representatives when needed. * Setting a standard of enthusiasm, accuracy and reliability that others can follow. * Keeping information secure and complying with the company’s Information Security Management System. **Who You Probably Are** * Someone who combines accuracy with speed — not one or the other. * Someone who can prioritise their own workload without waiting to be told what’s urgent. * Someone who knows the commercial implications of scheduling decisions and isn’t shy about saying so. * Someone who understands that good communication prevents expensive misunderstandings. **Why this role matters:** The Electrical QS has more influence on project outcomes than most people realise. When you do your job well, the company delivers better margins, fewer disputes and cleaner final accounts. When you don’t, things get messy. We’re hiring someone who takes pride in keeping things un‑messy.

title: Engineers – Engineering, Asset Management & Offshore
description: We are at the heart of Ireland’s energy transition \- connecting, operating and transforming the electricity grid to support a low carbon, renewable future. We are building talented teams that tackle real world challenges every day and help us to achieve our goal to prepare the grid to carry up to 80% of Ireland’s electricity from renewable sources. We are proud to be a certified Great Place to Work, recognised for our culture built on trust, collaboration and inclusion. At EirGrid you will find supportive teams, opportunities to grow and meaningful work. We also offer secure employment with a range of flexible and family friendly working policies to support your life and wellbeing. If you want to know more about what EirGrid has to offer you, visit If you are ready to make an impact and grow your career in a purpose\-led organisation, your journey starts here. **The Opportunity** We now seeking Engineers to join our Engineering \& Asset Management (E\&AM). We have variety of new roles from junior engineers to senior lead engineers for the following areas: Client Engineering, Asset Management and Offshore. The successful candidates will perform engineering assignments to ensure that business priorities are met. The client engineering function is responsible for undertaking project design reviews/site inspections on both contestable and non\-contestable transmission projects to ensure that projects are developed in accordance with EirGrid functional specification standards. Delivery of these projects is required to meet key departmental and company objectives. **The Team** The Engineering \& Asset Management is responsible for undertaking four key roles; asset management, technical authority, onshore engineering and offshore engineering. The team is responsible for setting the technical engineering policies and standards to be used for transmission infrastructure, monitoring compliance during design and construction and for ensuring asset integrity and ensuring uninterrupted power system functionality. The Engineering and Asset Management and Offshore Delivery are the areas within EirGrid which facilitates that role through the development of technical standards and functional specifications for all plant to be connected to the transmission system and the development of new and innovative technologies and methodologies. In addition, these areas are responsible for ensuring that EirGrid’s requirements are incorporated in project design and construction whether the works are carried out by ESB Networks or by third parties. **About You** Successful candidates will have: * Electrical Engineering qualification to degree level or equivalent qualification, typically with 4\+ years relevant post\-qualification experience. * Good technical knowledge in high voltage transmission design with strong knowledge of transmission technology including new and emerging technologies. * Transmission Substation design expertise with experience assessing substation designs for conformity to standards at 110/220/400 kV. * Excellent communication skills with an emphasis on high quality report writing and ability. * Experience preparing and issuing transmission design drawings, such as single line diagrams, substation layout drawings, protection schematics and interlocking drawings, etc * Experience completing onsite inspections and monitoring of construction progress for compliance. * Full drivers licence. **Closing date for applications** 22nd May 2026 *Please note we receive a high volume of applications, and as every application is carefully reviewed our response time may vary. If you agree to be considered for other roles this may result in a delayed response as we establish the best fit for your experience. We appreciate your patience.* We value potential as much as experience. If you believe you can succeed in this role, we encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every requirement. We're dedicated to diversity and inclusion at EirGrid. We recognise the strength that comes from having a diverse workforce and the importance of a supportive culture for all our people to achieve their potential. Our diversity and inclusion networks have been designed to create an inclusive culture that enables our people to feel a sense of belonging at work. As an equal opportunities’ employer, we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. Reasonable accommodations are offered at every stage of our recruitment process.

title: Enterprise Solution Architect
description: At Vanta, our mission is to help businesses earn and prove trust. We believe that security should be monitored and verified continuously, and we empower companies to practice better security and prove it with ease. Vanta has a kind and talented team, and while some have prior security experience, many have been successful at Vanta without it. As a Solution Architect at Vanta, you will play a pivotal role in securing the technical win and designing a solutions blueprint for our largest and most strategic global Enterprise prospects. Our team is dedicated to ensuring our Enterprise prospects can achieve seamless and successful implementations by validating their technical fit, identifying technical risks, and capitalising on opportunities for custom technical solutions. We strive to provide exceptional technical guidance and support to unblock deals and drive revenue growth. You will be the technical lead for our Enterprise deals, working closely with the Solutions Engineering team to understand their requirements, design solutions, and oversee technical validation. You will collaborate with cross\-functional teams, including Sales, Product, Engineering, and Customer Success, to ensure our clients’ success. **What you’ll do as a Solutions Architect at Vanta:** * Partner with Enterprise AEs and AMs to secure the technical win on Vanta’s most complex, strategic deals. * Lead Discovery and Architecture Workshops to deep\-dive into prospect tech stacks and identify technical blockers. * Collaborate with account teams to prove technical feasibility and eliminate risk. * Provide expert guidance on Vanta’s platform and API capabilities, best practices, and integration strategies. * Design comprehensive Technical Success Plans that map Vanta’s platform to complex customer architectures. * Act as the ‘Voice of the CISO’ by consolidating strategic product gaps and collaborating with Product and Engineering teams to influence product enhancements and address strategic feedback. **How to be successful in this role:** * Minimum of 5\+ years of technical experience, as well as 3\+ years being in a pre\-sales role as a Solution Architect, Sales Engineer, or similar pre\-sales technical role, preferably within the security or compliance industry supporting large Enterprise customers. * You have a solid understanding of the application development lifecycle and RESTful API design concepts, with considerable experience in scripting against APIs or building API\-based integrations. * Strong technical background, with proficiency in security protocols, cloud infrastructure, and software integration. * You thrive in dynamic environments where ambiguity is common * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to articulate complex technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders. * Excellent project management skills and proven track record of successfully managing complex technical sales cycles, running Proof of Concepts (POCs), and driving technical validation. * Understanding of sales methodologies (e.g., MEDDPICC) and how to map technical solutions to business value. * Ability to work collaboratively with cross\-functional teams and manage multiple projects simultaneously. * Experience in GRC (Governance, Risk, and Compliance) is a strong plus. * Relevant certifications (e.g., CISSP, CISM, AWS Certified Solutions Architect/Azure Solution Architect Professional/Google Professional Cloud Architect) are a plus. * Proficiency in one or more of the following languages is a plus: Python, Java, C\#, Ruby, or .NET. * Open to using AI to amplify their skills and strengthen their work \- demonstrating curiosity, a willingness to learn, and sound judgment in applying AI responsibly to improve efficiency and impact. **What you can expect as a Vanta’n:** * Industry\-competitive salary and equity * 100% covered medical, dental, and vision benefits with dependents coverage * 16 weeks paid Parental Leave for all new parents * Health \& wellness stipend * Remote workspace, internet, and mobile phone stipend * Commuter benefits for team members who attend the office * Pension matching * 25 days of Annual Leave per year and unlimited sick time * 8 company\-paid holidays * Virtual team building activities, lunch and learns, and other company\-wide events! * Offices in SF, NYC, London, Dublin, Tel Aviv, and Sydney *At Vanta, we are committed to hiring diverse talent of different backgrounds and as such, it is important to us to provide an inclusive work environment for all. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender identity, age, religion, sexual orientation, veteran or disability status, or any other protected class. As an equal opportunity employer, we encourage and welcome people of all backgrounds to apply.* **About Vanta** We started in 2018, in the wake of several high\-profile data breaches. Online security was only becoming more important, but we knew firsthand how hard it could be for fast\-growing companies to invest the time and manpower it takes to build a solid security foundation. Vanta was inspired by a vision to restore trust in internet businesses by enabling companies to improve and prove their security. From our early days automating security monitoring for compliance standards like SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO 27001 to creating the world's leading Trust Management Platform, our vision remains unchanged. Now more than ever, making security continuous—not just a point\-in\-time check— is essential. Thousands of companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust— all in a way that's real\-time and transparent. **Referral Instructions** If you are being referred for the role, please contact that person to apply on your behalf.

title: Financial Accountant
description: **Location \-** Dublin/Leeds/London **Hybrid \-** 2 days per week (Dublin only) **Contract Type \-** 2 year FTC Flutter consists of two commercial divisions (FanDuel and International) and our central Flutter Functions; COO, Finance \& Legal. Here in Flutter Functions, we work with colleagues across all our divisions and regions to deliver something we call the Flutter Edge. It’s our competitive advantage, our ‘secret sauce’ which plays a key part in our ongoing success and powers our brands and divisions, through Product, Tech, Expertise and Scale. In Flutter Finance we pride ourselves in providing global expertise to ensure Flutter is financially healthy. Utilizing our Flutter Edge to turbo\-charge our capabilities. We are looking for a Financial Accountant in our Record to Report (RTR) space, that will deliver financial and management information to all internal and external stakeholders. This role will focus predominantly on accounting and reporting for OpEx costs within the GFC team, across Flutter Functions \& UKI. This will include being involved in the management of specific operating expenses and the reporting of monthly results to the business, along with analysis. **What You’ll Do** * Assist with the production of month end numbers for Flutter Functions \& UKI, within Group Reporting deadlines * Operate a robust cost accruals process to ensure that costs are correctly accrued within the time constraints of a fast\-close environment * Review all significant variances for areas of responsibility flagging key variances in advance of close * Standardise, and automate where possible, month end tasks to improve month end timelines * Continuously review processes to find efficiencies in ways of working and opportunities to implement AI initiatives * Complete balance sheet reconciliations within set deadlines * Proactively engage with assigned budget owners to improve processes and procedures around monthly accruals * Continuously review processes and procedures in line with changes in systems and the requirements of the business * Ensure processes and procedures are documented and robust * Proactively engage in cross training initiatives * Other tasks that may be assigned as appropriate * Work collaboratively with related finance teams to achieve wider finance/organisational goals and efficiencies * Pursue opportunities to reduce the monthly close process duration without impacting the quality of the information produced * Ensure processes and procedures within area of responsibility are documented and robust * Work with all teams within the GFC as part of accounts production / close process to ensure accurate and timely accounting information is produced in accordance with their requirements * Support Commercial Finance in the budget and forecasting process to meet Group timelines * Proactively engage with internal and external audits when required * Deliver timely and accurate financial information to the Group reporting team **How You’ll Do It** To succeed in this role, you should have: * A qualification as an accountant with 2\+ years working in a similar environment, demonstrating a proven track record in financial reporting and analysis * Keen attention to detail \& drive to improve quality * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to challenge stakeholders and take ownership of your personal development * The ability to explain financial concepts to non\-financial staff confidently and professionally * Comfort working with large volumes of data and in highly automated environments * A team\-oriented approach with the ability to thrive in a dynamic environment * Experience with Oracle ERP or other similar financial systems an advantage * Experience in a US GAAP/SOx environment (advantageous) * Excellent proficiency in MS Office, particularly Excel **What’s In It For You** We are a flexible employer; whether you have personal commitments or a hobby that brings you joy, we want you to bring your best self to work and feel empowered to do so. We also like to share our success; after all you make it happen. We have an excellent benefits package that can be personalised to you: * Bonus scheme * Uncapped holiday allowance * Enhanced pension scheme * Private healthcare * Life assurance * Income protection * Hybrid working * £/€1,000 annual self\-development learning fund * Invest via the Flutters Sharesave Scheme * Paid volunteering days * Enhanced parental leave * Wellbeing fund (£/€250 a year) * Recognition programs * Electric car scheme, gym membership, discounts, vouchers and much more! **About Flutter** We are a world leader in online sports betting and iGaming, with a market leading position in the US and across the world. We have an unparalleled portfolio of the most innovative, diverse and distinctive brands including FanDuel, Sky Betting \& Gaming, Sportsbet, PokerStars, Paddy Power, Sisal, tombola, Betfair, MaxBet, Junglee Games and Adjarabet. With our global scale and challenger attitude, through which we excite and entertain our customers, in a safe and sustainable way. Using our collective power, the Flutter Edge, we aim to disrupt the sector, learning from the past to create a better future for our customers, colleagues and communities. We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role. If you need any adjustments to make this role work for you let us know, and we’ll see how we can accommodate them.

title: Financial and Regulatory Reporting Analyst
description: **Job Title** : Financial and Regulatory Reporting Analyst **Business Unit** : BNP Paribas Security (BP2S) Operations **Contract Type** : Permanent **Location** : Sandyford, Dublin/Parkmore, Galway (Hybrid) **Why work with us?** We are the bank for a changing world and we aim to build positive change together: * We are a team of colleagues who are enthusiastic about what we do and the impact our work has on our clients. We work in a highly collaborative, open\-minded environment that embraces innovation and creativity + We offer competitive remuneration and a comprehensive benefits package, including a discretionary bonus scheme, company pension contribution, comprehensive private healthcare packages, health and fitness subsidy, work\-from\-home subsidy, above statutory days of annual leave and numerous other benefits. + We are strongly committed to wellbeing and work\-life balance, including a 50% hybrid work model (where the role allows), paid family leave, fertility and menopause support — available to support all life stage needs. + Equality, diversity and inclusion are at the core of our culture, and we actively promote equal opportunities through creating or being part of initiatives and programmes: + Aspire Female Leadership Programme, provides development and exposure to high\-potential women in the bank, through targeted workshops, executive coaching and formal mentoring + Proud members of 100 Women in Finance and among the first signatories of Ireland’s Women in Finance charter + Joined the \#JamaisSansElles (Never Without Her) Charter, committing its executive committee to only join public or media events with panels of three or more speakers, if at least one of those speakers is a woman + Development and mobility are actively encouraged. BNP Paribas’ culture is built on purposeful talent development and recognition programmes that foster engagement, inclusive leadership and continuous growth. A few of our programmes include: + Leaders for Tomorrow, a programme that fast\-tracks high\-potential talent through training, development and cross\-functional projects, preparing them for future leadership positions + Local and Global Mentoring initiatives, that pair employees with senior leaders, providing staff with platforms to gain advice on career growth topics + Ambassador Awards employee recognition scheme provides rewards to those who have embodied the Bank’s core values + Local Experts (Champions) Programme, identifying internal subject\-matter experts from specific business areas, who excel at their craft + We are a global company operating in over 60 countries with over 180,000 employees from over 150 nationalities. In Ireland we have approximately 550 employees across Dublin and Galway, working in Corporate Institutional Banking (CIB) and BNP Paribas Security Services (BP2S) and Greenval **Equality and Diversity** BNP Paribas is an equal opportunities employer and proudly cultivates a diverse workforce. We believe that diversity of experience enhances our service offering to clients and leads to a better working environment. We welcome applications from the best candidates regardless of age, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, socio\-economic background, parental and caring status, or religious belief. Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) play a key role in promoting diversity and inclusion through initiatives, events and advocacy programmes that foster a sense of belonging for all colleagues. In BNP Paribas Ireland, we have dedicated networks for parents and caregivers, LGBTQA\+, staff from diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, alongside forums to discuss CSR and gender related topics. **Sustainable Finance** BNP continues to demonstrate the Bank’s steadfast commitment to supporting its clients in their growth ambitions by expanding market reach across assets, delivering excellence in bond markets, and showing the way in sustainable financing. **Role** * This role sits in the BNP Paribas Securities Services division (BP2S) * As a key member of the team, the objective of this role is to assist in the production of the financial and regulatory reporting obligations of the funds we administer * The core responsibilities require some subject matter expertise with attention to detail, quality control and excellent service delivery * The team works closely with the Operations functions to ensure successful delivery of financial and regulatory reporting deliverables * It is an exciting opportunity for someone with experience in fund administration and an interest in financial/regulatory reporting to be involved with a growing service within our Fund Administration business **K** **ey Responsibilities** * To assist in production of financial and regulatory reporting team obligations (Financial Reporting and Regulatory Reporting) * Ensure all financial reporting procedures are adhered to and ensure compliance with internal and external regulations * To assist in efficiently planning the financial statement preparation and audit process in conjunction with all relevant parties, ensuring that agreed timelines are achieved * Assist with the workflows for all audit engagements, act as both an internal reference and escalation point for audit issues * To keep up to date with all relevant regulatory and accounting changes and assist with re\-engineering of processes where necessary * To liaise with clients and auditors to ensure that the financial statements are audited and filed with the relevant regulatory bodies and Stock Exchanges on a timely basis * Assist with ensuring effective oversight of outsourcing vendors in accordance with Outsourcing Policy and to ensure high service levels are maintained * Preparation and review of client CBI regulatory returns (DIFR, MMIF, Monthly NAV return etc.) * Other ad hoc projects **Qualifications \& Experience Required For This Role** * Finance/Business graduate, Qualified/Part qualified Accountant (ACA, ACCA, CPA or CIMA) an advantage * Minimum of 3 years’ experience working in a financial reporting department, audit experience or fund administration roles * Understanding of accounting standards, primarily IFRS, US GAAP and FRS 102 a benefit * Be able to take responsibility and resolve issues as they arise * Ability to work to tight deadlines * Excellent organisational and Microsoft office skills * Knowledge of Fund Accounting/Investor Services or other accounting platforms an advantage * Excellent communication skills **About BNP Paribas** At BNP Paribas, we center our values on what drives the company forward. Our driving forces are: * Agility: we want to behave more simply to embrace useful innovation and digital transformation * Client Satisfaction: we believe that our success lies in being the customers’ and clients’ preferred choice. We seek to listen carefully to them and work closely with them * Compliance Culture: We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics * Openness: We promote open\-minded attitudes towards our stakeholders. We aim for everyone in Group to feel included, to have their say and be empowered **Our Strengths Are Based On Four Key Areas** * Stability: we build upon our solid, long\-term oriented management, our diversified and integrated business model and our international footprint * Expertise: We build upon our recognized and expanding knowledge of our teams * Responsibility: We build upon our culture of responsibility and integrity to ever better serve the interest of our customers * Good Place to Work: We foster a stimulating workplace where people are treated fairly and with respect **Management excellence** BNP continues to foster top\-class people management practices within our strong people focused culture. BNP Paribas managers follow the Group’s Management Charter, which emphasises a positive, empowering team culture, encouraging open communication and supporting development and wellbeing. **Compliance Culture** We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics. We would expect any incoming member of staff to respect all financial security related legislation, including anti\-money laundering (AML); countering the financing of terrorism (CFT); international financial sanctions and embargoes (IFS); anti\-bribery and corruption (ABC); KYC; ongoing client related data screening; and transaction monitoring. BNP Paribas have clear policies on these topics which all staff are expected to familiarise themselves with upon successful entry to the Group. Should you require any reasonable accommodation for the interview process, please let us know. By submitting your application, you agree to BNP Paribas completing a background check and screening in advance of any potential offer being made.

title: Full Stack Software Engineer II - Guest Engagement
description: Toast creates technology to help restaurants and local businesses succeed in a digital world, helping business owners operate, increase sales, engage customers, and keep employees happy. We’re seeking a Software Engineer to work within our team focused on building impactful capabilities that empower restaurants to drive Guest engagement, retention and growth. The team will help shape the future of how restaurants use technology to connect with their guests \- increasingly through AI\-powered tools and experiences that drive automation, personalization and smarter decision\-making. You will be a key part of a high\-impact, high\-ownership engineering team solving complex challenges in a fast\-moving product space. Your role will balance technical excellence alongside a strong product mindset to solve for continuous growth within both the team and our product space. **A Day In The Life (Responsibilities)** * Deliver projects that deeply connect restaurants with their guests within a cross\-functional collaborative and supportive environment. * Design, build, deploy and maintain highly resilient and scalable systems critical to our customers’ success. * Collaborate with team members and other engineers across the company. * Monitor feature rollouts to ensure customer success, iterating and improving when necessary. * Advocate for best coding practices, raising the bar for yourself, your team and the company. * Leverage cutting edge AI tools to enhance your development workflow, improve velocity, and help pioneer new approaches to building \- contributing to a culture of innovation and productivity across the team. **What You'll Need To Thrive (Requirements)** * Driven by impact and solving for the customer * Proficient in Java, Kotlin, or similar object\-oriented languages * Experience with modern web technologies and UI frameworks (e.g., React, Javascript , TypeScript, REST, GraphQL) * Enjoys building well\-engineered, usable and accessible systems * Ability to break down problems and communicate solutions clearly throughout cross\-functional teams. * Values personal and team growth, balancing task completion with platform stability. **Our Tech Stack** * A core of Java \& Kotlin based backend services. * React SPAs leveraging Tailwind, GraphQL and Storybook. * Android app built in Java \& Kotlin running in the restaurant. * PostgreSQL, Dynamodb for persistence. * Pulsar, Camel and RabbitMQ for message\-based integrations. * And many more technologies that help us build a top class platform. * This role requires a minimum of two days per week in our Dublin office\*\* **\#BI\-Hybrid** **AI at Toast** At Toast, one of our company values is that we're hungry to build and learn. We believe learning new AI tools empowers us to build for our customers faster, more independently, and with higher quality. We provide these tools across all disciplines, from Engineering and Product to Sales and Support, and are inspired by how our Toasters are already driving real value with them. The people who thrive here are those who embrace changes that let us build more for our customers; it’s a core part of our culture. **Our Total Rewards Philosophy** We strive to provide competitive compensation and benefits programs that help to attract, retain, and motivate the best and brightest people in our industry. Our total rewards package goes beyond great earnings potential and provides the means to a healthy lifestyle with the flexibility to meet Toasters’ changing needs. Learn more about our benefits at https://careers.toasttab.com/toast\-benefits. **How Toast Uses AI In Its Hiring Process** Throughout the hiring process, our goal is to get to know you. We use AI tools to support our recruiters and interviewers with tasks like note\-taking, summarization, and documentation of interviews to ensure they can be fully focused on your conversation. All hiring decisions are made by people. To learn more: https://careers.toasttab.com/ai\-in\-hiring ****Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion is Baked into our Recipe for Success**** At Toast, our employees are our secret ingredient—when they thrive, we thrive. The restaurant industry is one of the most diverse, and we embrace that diversity with authenticity, inclusivity, respect, and humility. By embedding these principles into our culture and design, we create equitable opportunities for all and raise the bar in delivering exceptional experiences. **We Thrive Together** We embrace a hybrid work model that fosters in\-person collaboration while valuing individual needs. Our goal is to build a strong culture of connection as we work together to empower the restaurant community. To learn more about how we work globally and regionally, check out: https://careers.toasttab.com/locations\-toast. **Apply today!** Toast is committed to creating an accessible and inclusive hiring process. As part of this commitment, we strive to provide reasonable accommodations for persons with disabilities to enable them to access the hiring process. If you need an accommodation to access the job application or interview process, please contact candidateaccommodations@toasttab.com. - *For roles in the United States, it is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.*

title: General Practitioner - Laya Healthcare Clinics Dublin (Full-Time)
description: **Locations** Cherrywood \& Swords, Dublin **Working Days** Cherrywood: Monday, Tuesday \& Thursday \| Swords: Wednesday and Friday \| **Hours** 10 sessions per week **Salary** Competitive – dependent on experience **Contract Type** Permanent \| Full\-Time across both sites or Part\-Time/Job\-Share considered in each site **Reports To** Chief Medical Officer **About the Partnership** Laya Healthcare and WebDoctor have partnered to deliver an innovative healthcare model that combines the reach of digital consultations with the depth of in\-person clinical care. Operating across two fully equipped clinic locations in Cherrywood and Swords, this partnership is redefining how private healthcare is delivered in Ireland. We are seeking an experienced Lead General Practitioner to join this exciting venture and play a central role in delivering high quality patient care across both clinic sites. **The Opportunity** This is a unique opportunity to join a forward\-thinking healthcare company at an exciting stage of growth. As a GP in our clinics, you can deliver high\-quality consultations to patients presenting with a wide variety of conditions, supported by a dedicated Practice Nurse, Practice Manager, and integrated clinical systems. **Key Responsibilities** LAYA and Webdoctor are dedicated to delivering integrated, patient\-centric primary care through our innovative care model. As Lead General Practitioner across our new clinics in Cherrywood and Swords, you will be instrumental in bridging the gap between cutting\-edge digital health services and high\-quality, in\-person clinical excellence. This role requires a commitment to comprehensive care, ensuring that every patient benefits from seamless continuity whether they are engaging remotely or in the clinics, supported by modern technology and dedicated clinical staff. **Clinical Care** * Provide core general medical services to patients within the safety limitations of the clinic environment. * Have input into and oversight of specialist clinical services as appropriate and agreed. * We welcome GPs who are keen to explore and develop specialist service areas within the clinics, contributing to the expansion of our clinical offering. * Video Consultations: Embrace and utilise Webdoctor's digital\-first approach by providing high\-quality, remote video consultations alongside in\-person care, ensuring seamless continuity of service for all patients. * Collaborate with the Practice Nurse and wider clinical team to manage chronic disease and implement preventative health strategies. **Clinical Leadership \& Quality** * The Lead GP will take on a pivotal clinical leadership role within the clinics, working closely with the Chief Medical Officer to maintain high clinical standards, ensure quality patient care, and support the growth of the service. **Clinical Governance \& Quality Oversight** * Take responsibility for leading and implementing the clinical governance agenda within the clinics, including the development and regular review of clinical protocols, performance data, and patient safety incidents (e.g., significant event analysis and complaints review). * Ensure adherence to all medical protocols, clinic policies, and clinical governance standards. * Participate in team meetings, clinical audits, and quality improvement initiatives. * Complete patient records accurately and promptly using the clinic’s electronic medical record system. **Team Collaboration \& Mentorship** * Work collaboratively with the Practice Nurse, Practice Manager, and wider clinical team to ensure efficient, high\-quality patient care. * Communicate effectively with patients, providing clear and empathetic explanations of conditions and treatment options. * Oversee the training and onboarding process for new clinical staff, including GPs, Practice Nurses, and other healthcare professionals. * Act as a mentor and guide to less experienced clinical team members, fostering a culture of continuous professional development and high clinical standards. * Provide clinical supervision, advice, and professional support to other GPs and allied healthcare professionals within the Webdoctor service. * Conduct staff onboarding, training, and performance appraisals. * Work with the digital healthcare team to develop and manage hybrid healthcare services, where appropriate. **Service Development \& Management Interface** * Actively contribute clinical expertise to the strategic development of new services and clinical pathways, ensuring innovative offerings meet regulatory requirements and patient needs. * Support the phased rollout of new clinical services as the clinics grow. * Serve as the primary clinical interface for the Practice Manager and operational team on day\-to\-day clinical issues, ensuring seamless integration between clinical service delivery and operational efficiency. **Person Specification** **Essential** * Specialist registration with the Irish Medical Council (IMC) * Experience in general practice – minimum 3 years post\-specialist training preferred * Strong clinical acumen across a broad range of presentations * Excellent communication skills with a compassionate, patient\-focused approach * Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team * Up\-to\-date knowledge of medical best practices, national guidelines, and relevant legislation * Right to work in Ireland * Ability to mentor and guide junior clinical staff, contributing to a culture of continuous learning and high title: General Practitioner - Laya Healthcare Clinics Dublin (Part-Time, Cherrywood)
description: **Locations** Cherrywood, Dublin **Working Days** Monday, Tuesday \& Thursday **Hours** 6 sessions per week **Salary** Competitive – dependent on experience **Contract Type** Permanent \| Part\-Time across one site or option available to work Full\-Time across two sites **Reports To** Chief Medical Officer **About the Partnership** Laya Healthcare and WebDoctor have partnered to deliver an innovative healthcare model that combines the reach of digital consultations with the depth of in\-person clinical care. Operating across two fully equipped clinic locations in Cherrywood and Swords, this partnership is redefining how private healthcare is delivered in Ireland. We are seeking an experienced Lead General Practitioner to join this exciting venture and play a central role in delivering exceptional patient care across both or one of our new clinic sites. **The Opportunity** This is a unique opportunity to join a forward\-thinking healthcare company at an exciting stage of growth. As a GP in one of our clinics, you can deliver high\-quality consultations to patients presenting with a variety of conditions, supported by a dedicated Practice Nurse, Practice Manager, and integrated clinical systems. **Key Responsibilities** LAYA and Webdoctor are dedicated to delivering integrated, patient\-centric primary care through our innovative care model. As a General Practitioner in our new clinic(s) Cherrywood, you will be instrumental in bridging the gap between cutting\-edge digital health services and high\-quality, in\-person clinical excellence. This role requires a commitment to comprehensive care, ensuring that every patient benefits from seamless continuity whether they are engaging remotely or in the clinic, supported by modern technology and dedicated clinical staff. **Clinical Care** * Provide core general medical services to patients within the safety limitations of the clinic environment. * Have input into and oversight of specialist clinical services as appropriate and agreed. * We welcome GPs who are keen to explore and develop specialist service areas within the practice, contributing to the expansion of our clinical offering. * Video Consultations: Embrace and utilise Webdoctor's digital\-first approach by providing high\-quality, remote video consultations alongside in\-person care, ensuring seamless continuity of service for all patients. * Ensure continuity of care for all patients, effectively managing the transition between virtual and in\-person consultations. * Collaborate with the Practice Nurse and wider clinical team to manage chronic disease and implement preventative health strategies. **Clinical Leadership \& Quality** * The Lead GP will take on a pivotal clinical leadership role within the clinic(s), working closely with the Chief Medical Officer to maintain high clinical standards, ensure quality patient care, and support the growth of the practice. **Clinical Governance \& Quality Oversight** * Take responsibility for leading and implementing the clinical governance agenda within the clinic(s), including the development and regular review of clinical protocols, performance data, and patient safety incidents (e.g., significant event analysis and complaints review). * Ensure adherence to all medical protocols, clinic policies, and clinical governance standards. * Participate in team meetings, clinical audits, and quality improvement initiatives. * Complete patient records accurately and promptly using the clinics’ electronic medical record system. **Team Collaboration \& Mentorship** * Work collaboratively with the Practice Nurse, Practice Manager, and wider clinical team to ensure efficient, high\-quality patient care. * Communicate effectively with patients, providing clear and empathetic explanations of conditions and treatment options. * Oversee the training and onboarding process for new clinical staff, including GPs, Practice Nurses, and other healthcare professionals. * Act as a mentor and guide to less experienced clinical team members, fostering a culture of continuous professional development and high clinical standards. * Provide clinical supervision, advice, and professional support to other GPs and allied healthcare professionals within the Webdoctor service. * Conduct staff onboarding, training, and performance appraisals as required. * Work with the digital healthcare team to develop and manage hybrid healthcare services, where appropriate. **Service Development \& Management Interface** * Actively contribute clinical expertise to the strategic development of new services and clinical pathways, ensuring innovative offerings meet regulatory requirements and patient needs. * Support the phased rollout of new clinical services as the clinic grows. * Serve as the primary clinical interface for the Practice Manager and operational team on day\-to\-day clinical issues, ensuring seamless integration between clinical service delivery and operational efficiency. **Person Specification** **Essential** * Specialist registration with the Irish Medical Council (IMC) * Experience in general practice – minimum 3 years post\-specialist training preferred * Strong clinical acumen across a broad range of presentations * Excellent communication skills with a compassionate, patient\-focused approach * Ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team * Up\-to\-date knowledge of medical best practices, national guidelines, and relevant legislation * Right to work in Ireland * Ability to mentor and guide junior clinical staff, contributing to a culture of continuous learning and high clinical standards * Experience in clinical governance processes, including leading clinical audits, reviewing significant events, and contributing to quality improvement initiatives * Demonstrable management and leadership skills, with experience in or willingness to take responsibility for operational clinical oversight and team coordination **Desirable** * Experience in or willingness to develop skills in specialist areas * Experience with digital health platforms * Interest in contributing to service development and clinical innovation **What We Offer** * Competitive salary based on experience with transparent progression * Structured working hours with no on\-call commitments * Pension contribution of 3% employer contribution after 6 months * Discounted Laya Health and Dental Insurance * Bike to work scheme * Travel Tax Saver * A modern, fully equipped clinic environment Supportive, collaborative team with dedicated Practice Nurse and Practice Manager * Opportunities for Continuing Professional Development (CPD) and specialist training which will be influenced by service needs. * A genuine opportunity to shape the growth of new clinics and influence service delivery **How to Apply** If you are a skilled and motivated GP looking for a rewarding role in a modern, patient\-centred clinic environment, we would love to hear from you. Please submit your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience, areas of clinical interest, and suitability for the role.
