title: Graduate Sustainability Engineer
description: **About The Role – Graduate Sustainability Engineer** As a Graduate Sustainability Engineer at Ethos Engineering, you will play a crucial role within our Sustainability Group, which focuses on advancing environmentally responsible building practices and certifications. You will have the opportunity to work on exciting projects and contribute to the design and implementation of sustainable building systems, including involvement with green building certifications such as HPI and LEED. **Candidate Attributes** * Candidate must be based in Ireland to be considered for this position. * Bachelor’s degree in Engineering/ Environmental/ Sustainability, Building Service, Architecture or a related field. * A strong interest in sustainability and green building practices. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and attention to detail. * Strong communication and teamwork abilities. * Knowledge of sustainability certifications, particularly HPI and LEED, is a plus. * Familiarity with sustainability assessment tools and software is desirable. **Role Requirements** * Collaborate with the Sustainability Group to develop and implement sustainable design strategies in building projects. * Gain hands\-on experience with certifications such as HPI (Home Performance Index) and LEED (Leadership in Energy and Environmental Design). * Contribute to the assessment of building energy performance and recommend energy\-saving solutions. * Stay up\-to\-date with industry trends and best practices related to sustainable design and green building certifications. * Analyse data related to sustainability metrics, energy consumption, and environmental impact, providing insights to enhance project sustainability. * Collaborate with clients to understand their sustainability goals and help translate them into actionable plans. * Assist in the preparation of reports, presentations, and documentation required for green building certification submissions. * Work closely with multidisciplinary project teams to integrate sustainable design principles into engineering solutions. **Why Join Us?** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal wellbeing** . **We offer:** * Flexible Fridays and a 9\-day fortnight working schedule — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A culture that values flexibility, learning, and teamwork, with clear progression pathways and support toward professional accreditation. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the most experienced engineers in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times / Hybrid Working * 20 days of holidays, increasing up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Your own company laptop * Coverage for working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training, mentoring, and further college support * Support for Chartership * A professional subscription fee paid by the company * Heavily subsidized social committee activities (5\-a\-side football, golf society, climbing club, and regular social events) Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. **At Ethos, our *vision* is to:** ***“Be the company people want to work with, and for”*** **Values** Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be **EPIC** \- for our people and our customers. Recruitment Agencies: Please do not reply to this job advert. Should Ethos require assistance with this posting you will be contacted by our Talent Acquisition Team.

title: Guest Experience Supervisor
description: **Additional Information** **Job Number**26056732 **Job Category**Rooms \& Guest Services Operations **Location**The Shelbourne Autograph Collection, 27 St. Stephen's Green, Dublin, Dublin, Ireland **Schedule**Full Time **Located Remotely?**N **Position Type** Non\-Management **POSITION SUMMARY** Process all guest check\-ins, verifying guest identity, form of payment, assigning room, and activating/issuing room key. Set up accurate accounts for each guest according to their requirements. Enter Marriott Rewards information. Ensure rates match market codes, document exceptions. Secure payment prior to issuing room key, verify/adjust billing. Compile and review daily reports/logs/contingency lists. Complete cashier and closing reports. Supply guests with directions and property information. Accommodate guest requests, contacting appropriate staff if necessary. Follow up to ensure requests have been met. Process all payment types, vouchers, paid\-outs, and charges. Balance and drop receipts. Count and secure bank at beginning and end of shift. Obtain manual authorizations and follow all Accounting procedures. Notify Loss Prevention/Security of any guest reports of theft. Assist management in training, evaluating, counseling, motivating and coaching employees; serve as a role model and first point of contact of the Guarantee of Fair Treatment/Open Door Policy process. Develop/maintain positive working relationships; support team to reach common goals; listen and respond appropriately to employee concerns. Follow company policies and procedures; report accidents, injuries, and unsafe work conditions to manager; complete safety training and certifications; ensure personal appearance is clean and professional; maintain confidentiality of proprietary information; protect company assets. Welcome and acknowledge guests; anticipate and address guests’ service needs; assist individuals with disabilities; thank guests with genuine appreciation. Speak using clear and professional language; prepare and review written documents accurately and completely; answer telephones using appropriate etiquette. Ensure adherence to quality standards. Enter and locate information using computers/POS systems. Stand, sit, or walk for an extended period of time. Move, lift, carry, push, pull, and place objects weighing less than or equal to 10 pounds without assistance. Perform other reasonable job duties as requested by Supervisors. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Education: High school diploma or G.E.D. equivalent. Related Work Experience: At least 1 year of related work experience. Supervisory Experience: At least 1 year of supervisory experience. License or Certification: None *At Marriott International, we are dedicated to being an equal opportunity employer, welcoming all and providing access to opportunity. We actively foster an environment where the unique backgrounds of our associates are valued and celebrated. Our greatest strength lies in the rich blend of culture, talent, and experiences of our associates. We are committed to non\-discrimination on any protected basis, including disability, veteran status, or other basis protected by applicable law.* The Autograph Collection is a growing collection of four and five\-star independent hotels, each offering unique and memorable experiences. Each hotel is thoughtfully chosen for its quality, originality, bold character, and ability to offer today’s traveler a range of unique experiences suited to their individual sense of style and adventure. All Autograph Collection hotels look to attract The Individualist, the brand's target consumer. The Individualist is someone who uses travel as an opportunity to add to their personal narrative – in essence they are hand selecting our hotels whether for personal or corporate travel because it is much more than simply a place to rest. In joining Autograph Collection, you join a portfolio of brands with Marriott International. **Be** where you can do your best work, **begin** your purpose, **belong** to an amazing global team, and **become** the best version of you.

title: IT Systems Support Analyst - Full time
description: **Job Description** **Location:** Roadstone, Cookstown Road, Fortunestown ISAC Ltd is currently seeking an experienced and technically capable IT Systems Support Analyst. This is a permanent position which will report to the Business Systems Manager where you will play a key role in the day\-to\-day support of business applications, incident management and hardware services across the organisation. This role will be based at Fortunestown, Tallaght, Dublin 24\. ISAC Ltd is a Shared Services Company within the CRH Group which provides a centralised Finance and Administration Function for fellow CRH Group companies (Roadstone, Northstone etc.) **Benefits** * Competitive Salary * Pension Scheme with up to 10% employer contribution * Share Participation Scheme (Revenue Approved) * Paid Holidays (22 days) * Training \& Development Opportunities. * Employee Assistance Programme * Free parking * Product discount * Bike\-to\-Work Scheme * Paid Maternity/Paternity Leave and other Family Leave available. **About The Role** **Key Areas of Responsibility:** **Application Support** * Provide day\-to\-day support for key business applications used across the organisation * Execute and monitor critical system processes and scheduled activities * Assist with system upgrades, patching lifecycles, and testing including user acceptance testing * Act as a primary liaison between third\-party vendors and business users, translating technical issues into clear, non\-technical language * Develop, maintain, and continuously improve system documentation, procedures, and support materials **Incident Management** * Monitor and manage incident and service request queues in collaboration with IT support teams * Track and manage SLAs using incident management tools, ensuring timely resolution of issues * Build and maintain KPI reports and dashboards to measure performance and identify trends * Support root cause analysis and contribute to continuous service improvement initiatives **Hardware Management** * Provide hands\-on hardware support across all business units * Build, image, configure, and deploy laptops, desktops, and peripheral equipment * Support hardware lifecycle activities including new starter onboarding and hardware refreshes * Assist with asset tracking and hardware documentation **The following knowledge, skills and competencies are required for this role:** **Essential** * Minimum of 3 years’ experience in an IT Analyst, IT Support, or similar role * Experience providing application and end\-user support in a corporate environment * Working knowledge of SQL Developer or similar database tools * Understanding of networking fundamentals, including connectivity troubleshooting * Experience supporting Azure\-based environments including Azure Virtual Desktop and/or Citrix * Strong troubleshooting skills across applications, desktops, printers, and user access * Excellent analytical, organisational, and planning skills * Ability to build strong working relationships with business users, management, vendors, and consultants **Desirable** * Experience supporting ERP / Dispatch systems * Exposure to IT compliance and control frameworks such as SOX * Project delivery experience or project management certification * Experience with Power Automate or workflow automation tools **Personal Attributes** * Analytical and detail\-oriented with a strong sense of ownership * Proactive problem solver with strong follow\-through * Confident communicator with technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Able to manage multiple priorities and work independently * Curious, collaborative, and comfortable learning new systems **Why Join Us?** * Opportunity to work across a broad range of applications, systems, and technologies * Hands\-on role with real ownership and visibility across the business * Collaborative IT environment with structured processes * Scope to develop your technical expertise and progress your IT career in a multinational environment * Hybrid role The successful candidate must be a highly motivated, self\-directed individual with a desire for personal, Company and Group achievement. **Closing date: close of business 20th of May 2026** **Skills Needed** **About The Company** Roadstone’s nationwide business includes the manufacture and distribution of a wide range of construction products including ready\-mix concrete, aggregates, asphalt and macadam, mortar and plaster, concrete blocks and masonry, paving products, roof tiles and agricultural lime. We also provide an asphalt contracting service for commercial, domestic and local authority road projects. Our business leaders include internally developed operational managers, highly qualified professionals and owner\-entrepreneurs who join the organisation as a result of acquisitions. The sustained expansion of CRH as a group, since its foundation in 1970, has created a wide range of career opportunities for ambitious and talented people throughout the world. Our decentralised structure and entrepreneurial environment is particularly suited to attracting, motivating and retaining talented, committed, enthusiastic and well\-qualified people. **Company Culture** Our company is committed to leadership and skills development at all levels of the organisation, encouraging and supporting the continuous professional development of its employees to achieve their potential. Our development philosophy involves leveraging challenging work assignments with targeted coaching and mentoring, augmented by formal training and education. Our Success is built on your Success Roadstone offers exciting and rewarding opportunities for talented, dedicated people. There’s no single type of person who succeeds in Roadstone. We are looking for people who have a passion for excellence and achieving results. You’ll need the ability to challenge and influence others, along with the capacity to adapt to new and different situations. The ability to build strong and effective relationships and to inspire and motivate others. **Required Criteria** **Desired Criteria** **Closing Date** Wednesday 20th May, 2026 **Contract Type** fulltime **Salary** Based on Experience

title: Information Technology Technical Support
description: **IT Service \& Support Technician** **Fixed Term Contract** **Dublin** **€40\-50K** Are you a tech\-savvy IT professional who thrives on solving problems and delivering great customer experiences? We have an exciting Fixed Term Contract opportunity for an IT Service \& Support Technician to join a busy, dynamic IT team. **The Role** You'll be the go\-to person for diagnosing, troubleshooting and resolving technical issues across desktop, mobile and network environments. You'll take full ownership of issues from first contact through to resolution , working across phone, email and chat with internal customers. **What You'll Do** You'll provide hands\-on support across Windows, Mac and Linux environments, manage tickets through systems such as ServiceNow or ConnectWise, support Google Workspace and mobile devices (iPhone/iPad), and liaise with wider IT teams to identify root causes for recurring issues. Creating and maintaining clear technical documentation will also be part of your day\-to\-day. **What You'll Need** ✅ Minimum 2 years' experience in a similar IT support role ✅ 3rd level degree in Computer Science, IT, Software Engineering or a related field ✅ Hands\-on experience with Windows, Linux and Mac OS environments ✅ Strong knowledge of computer systems, mobile devices and networking ✅ Experience using a ticketing system (ServiceNow, ConnectWise or similar) ✅ Excellent problem\-solving and communication skills, written and verbal ✅ A full clean driver's licence, **this is essential** ✅ Microsoft, Cisco or Linux certification is a bonus **What's on Offer** This role comes with a strong benefits package including private medical insurance, company pension, flexible start and finish times, up to 2 days remote working per week, on\-site gym and canteen, free parking and excellent career progression opportunities. **Interested?** If this sounds like your next move, I'd love to hear from you. Reach out in confidence to: margaret@mpgrecruitment.ie

title: Join AECOM's Quantity Surveyor Talent Network
description: **Company Description** **Work with Us. Change the World.** At AECOM, we're delivering a better world. Whether improving your commute, keeping the lights on, providing access to clean water, or transforming skylines, our work helps people and communities thrive. We are the world's trusted infrastructure consulting firm, partnering with clients to solve the world’s most complex challenges and build legacies for future generations. There has never been a better time to be at AECOM. With accelerating infrastructure investment worldwide, our services are in great demand. We invite you to bring your bold ideas and big dreams and become part of a global team of over 50,000 planners, designers, engineers, scientists, digital innovators, program and construction managers and other professionals delivering projects that create a positive and tangible impact around the world. We're one global team driven by our common purpose to deliver a better world. Join us. **Job Description** **Start here. Grow here.** **Join Our Growing Quantity Surveying Team at AECOM Ireland** At AECOM, we take pride in our impressive and expansive Quantity Surveying team, delivering excellence across Ireland. As our operations continue to grow, so too does our need for talented, dedicated, and passionate professionals who are ready to make a difference. Whether you're early in your career or a seasoned Quantity Surveyor, we would be delighted to hear from you. If you're looking for an opportunity to work on impactful projects with a collaborative and supportive team, AECOM could be the perfect fit. **Ready to take the next step in your career?** Get in touch — we’d love to hear from you. **Why Join AECOM?** * **Impactful Projects:** Our construction project management team leads some of the largest commercial,healthcare,education,data center and infrastructure projects across Ireland. By joining us, you'll deliver designs that prioritise innovative and sustainable solutions, working on collaborative platforms and harnessing cutting\-edge technology. * **Professional Growth:** At AECOM, we prioritise the professional growth of our team members from day one. We offer fast\-track opportunities for development, making you a key member of a diverse, dynamic, and ever\-expanding team. * **Flexibility:** Given the varied and dynamic nature of our projects, we regularly require different skill sets and levels of experience. We're flexible and often create roles around specific skill sets to best suit our team and projects. * **Sector experience/workload** \- Commercial,healthcare,education,data center and infrastructure sectors * **Locations:** Dublin,Cork,Galway and Belfast **Apply Now – Quantity Surveyors (All Levels)** **Location: Ireland \| AECOM** If you have a background in Quantity Surveying, we’d love to hear from you. Please submit your CV against this role — your application will be considered for current openings as well as upcoming opportunities across our expanding team. **Who We're Looking For** We’re seeking experienced professionals for roles ranging from **Quantity Surveyor** to **Associate Quantity Surveyor** . You’ll ideally bring experience in one or more of the following sectors: * Healthcare * Commercial * Data Centres * Infrastructure * Rai and Metro * Education At AECOM, you’ll be part of a dynamic, supportive, and forward\-thinking team delivering high\-impact projects across Ireland. If you’re ready to take the next step in your career, apply today. **Come grow with us.** At AECOM, you’ll thrive in an inclusive and forward\-thinking environment where your contributions are valued \- and let's not forget the perks! You'll enjoy a range of core and personalised benefits designed to support your future and well\-being, which you can tailor to fit your lifestyle. Take advantage of career development opportunities, technical practice networks, AECOM University, volunteering days and our flexible hybrid working model to create a healthy work\-life balance. **Qualifications** **Ready to push the limits of what’s possible?** **Here’s What We’re Looking For** As a successful candidate, you will have experience working within cost management in one or more of our key sectors. We're looking for individuals who can bring strong technical knowledge, commercial awareness, and a collaborative mindset. **Qualifications \& Experience:** * A degree in Quantity Surveying or a related discipline (or equivalent) * Chartered status with MRICS (or equivalent), or actively working towards accreditation * Demonstrated experience in cost management within sectors such as healthcare, commercial, data centres, infrastructure, or education * Strong communication and stakeholder management skills * Ability to work both independently and as part of a team on high\-profile projects **Additional Information** Interested in the role or curious about life at AECOM? Follow us on LinkedIn, Facebook, Instagram, and YouTube to explore our AECOM voices, employee stories, latest projects, and much more! For further information about the role, reach out to the recruiter on LinkedIn Colum Calnan **About AECOM** AECOM is the world’s trusted infrastructure consulting firm, delivering professional services throughout the project lifecycle – from advisory, planning, design and engineering to program and construction management. On projects spanning transportation, buildings, water, new energy and the environment, our public\- and private\-sector clients trust us to solve their most complex challenges. Our teams are driven by a common purpose to deliver a better world through our unrivaled technical and digital expertise, a culture of equity, diversity and inclusion, and a commitment to environmental, social and governance priorities. AECOM is a Fortune 500 firm and its Professional Services business had revenue of $16\.1 billion in fiscal year 2025\. See how we are delivering sustainable legacies for generations to come at aecom.com and @AECOM. **Freedom to Grow in a World of Opportunity** You will have the flexibility you need to do your best work with hybrid work options. Whether you’re working from an AECOM office, remote location or at a client site, you will be working in a dynamic environment where your integrity, entrepreneurial spirit and pioneering mindset are championed. You will help us foster a safe and respectful workplace, where we invite everyone to bring their whole selves to work using their unique talents, backgrounds and expertise to create transformational outcomes for our clients. AECOM provides a wide array of compensation, benefits and well\-being programs to meet the diverse needs of our employees and their families. We’re the world’s trusted global infrastructure firm, and we’re in this together – your growth and success are ours too. Join us, and you’ll get all the benefits of being a part of a global, publicly traded firm – access to industry\-leading technology and thinking and transformational work with big impact and work flexibility. As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we believe in each person’s potential, and we’ll help you reach yours. We are a Disability Confident Employer and will offer an interview to applicants who have a disability or long\-term condition, who meet the minimum/essential criteria for the role. Please let us know using this email address ReasonableAccommodations@aecom.com if you would like to apply through the Disability Confident Interview Scheme. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines. **ReqID:** REF46059N **Business Line:** PCC **Business Group:** DCS **Strategic Business Unit:** Europe \& India **Career Area:** Survey \& Estimation **Work Location Model:** Hybrid

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: Lab Analyst (6 month Contract)

Lab Analyst have the following responsibilities: * Accurately completing assigned analytical tests, examinations and procedures * Diligently completing all necessary quality records * Maintaining a clean and safe work environment by performing tasks in an organised and consistent fashion * Performing such other technical duties * Participate in such further training * Adhere to Health and Safety policy * Compliant to the ISO 17025, in relation to the testing, sampling, and calibration to collect reliable results. * Handling and preparation of chemical\-based reagents/buffers. * Assist in reviewing and developing Standard Operating Procedures * Ability to direct own work based upon lab priorities and deadlines * Lysing and cleaning samples * Genotyping * Samples quality checks * Making management aware of any issue **Requirements** Necessary qualifications/ training/ experience Degree/Diploma in Science or Equivalent Successful completion of internal induction training, as specified by the Lab Manger and described in the relevant training manual * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment * Strong multitasking skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities effectively * Experience with DNA extraction methods is an advantage * Experience operating liquid handlers is highly desirable

title: Mechanical Engineer
description: Mechanical Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now **Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Key Role Responsibilities** **Team Management:** * Collaborate with engineers, and construction managers to develop comprehensive project plans and ensure delivery on schedule. * Collaborate with any other consultants, subcontractors, supervisors, planners, estimators, quantity surveyors and the general workforce involved in the project. * Liaise with clients and other 3rd parties as required, such as insurance companies, vendors, etc. * Develop comprehensive scope\-of\-works (SoW), task information deliverables lists (TIDP), and responsibility matrices (R\&R/ RACI) for Mechanical disciplines. * Raise any design\-related risks to planning, commercial and management, and establish a course to minimise their impact. * Maintain accurate records of key design activities (e.g. changes, issues) as they arise and escalate to management as appropriate. * Assist the Central Engineering and site teams with proactive contributions to construction and team matters. * Attend design related meetings and provide continual input for optimisation. * Collaborate on new design issues/ changes/ research within the team. * Assist with new tenders and PCSA (pre\-construction services agreements) deliverables within the Business Unit. **Project Management \& Planning** * Oversee the construction process to ensure adherence to design specifications and schedules. * Assist in developing design delivery schedules along with the client, project manager, design team and consultants. * Assist with managing the deliverables and approvals processes of design packages to ensure full approval for procurement. * Check design programmes on projects are aligned to consultant resource and construction/ procurement requirements. * Conduct site visits and report progress and quality of work. * Correctly follow information control and flow standards e.g. for RFIs, use of ACC, etc. **Technical Engineering** * Ensure Mechanical technical compliance on all projects. * Ensure knowledge of client and country\-specific design standards. * Assess and mitigate potential risks and issues as they’re identified in design, or that could have potential impact on construction activities. * Review design proposals by others for suitability and constructability. * Offer proposals for added value (VE) and off\-site manufacturing (OSM). * Ensure material choices are aligned to project requirements, budget, and QEHS standards. * Prepare comprehensive handover packs to the site team for any packages under your responsibility. * Coordinate and assist with coordination of Mechanical disciplines and interfaces with CSA and Electrical. * Check plans, drawings, and quantities for accuracy of calculations. * Review, monitor, and interpret the contract design documents supplied by the client or architect. **Sustainability** * Integrate sustainable practices into design and construction processes. * Evaluate the environmental impact of projects and propose options for improvements. **Documentation** * Prepare detailed reports, and documentation for project approval and permits. * Maintain accurate records of project progress and changes. * Manage key registers throughout the project design period: + Procurement schedule. + TIDP/ MIDP. + RFI log. + Deviation tracker. + Lessons Learnt log. + Issues, interface \& scope gap trackers. + Design change tracker. + Value Engineering Register + OSM Register **Other** * Provide technical guidance and support to construction teams. * Review and submit technical submittals. * Assist with recruitment activities for the Central Engineering team as deemed suitable. * Undertake other engineering tasks or duties as required by the Engineering Manager and/ or Company. * The Company reserves the right to vary the scope and nature of the Employee’s normal duties to consider changes in the Company’s work patterns and practices and the requirements of its business. **Essential Criteria For The Role** * Minimum 5 years post graduate experience in the Industrial, Pharmaceutical, Life Sciences or Data Centre sectors. * Demonstration of design, technical and construction knowledge is essential for this role. * Experience in leading meetings and coordinating the other team members. * Strong working knowledge of design and analysis software such as BIM, Revit. * Clean driving licence essential **Mercury is an equal opportunities employer.** Apply Now

title: NeoDyne Engineering Graduate Programme Dublin 2026
description: *Want to influence the power grid of the future? Tackle climate change? Play your part in the greening of the gas network?* *Launch your career with NeoDyne and open a world of opportunities!* **Overview** NeoDyne is a leading engineering company specialising in industrial automation, digital manufacturing, and electrical engineering solutions. With a strong presence across multiple sectors, including Life Sciences, Food \& Beverage, and Energy, NeoDyne delivers leading\-edge solutions that drive efficiency, safety, and performance for its customers worldwide. We develop high\-availability automation and electrical systems for mission\-critical industries, including pharmaceutical manufacturing, data centres, and energy infrastructure, ensuring continuous operation and compliance. We additionally integrate emerging technologies to enhance control, scalability, and operational insight. Our expertise also extends to power generation, natural gas transmission, and high\-voltage system design, delivering robust solutions that strengthen critical infrastructure. With a team of 200\+ skilled engineers, our expertise spans the entire project lifecycle, from design and implementation to commissioning and operations. From our offices in Ireland, the UK, Romania, and the US, we operate on a global scale, combining technical expertise with local delivery. We support customers across Europe, North America, and Asia, delivering tailored engineering services that drive long\-term success. **Position Summary** NeoDyne are recruiting a **Graduate Electrical Engineer** to join our expanding engineering teams. Our graduate programme is all about learning new and developing your existing skills. Over a two\-year period, you will be placed in projects where you get to do meaningful work and contribute to the business. This Graduate opportunity is offered as a full\-time permanent contract, based at our **Dublin** **Office** , with an excellent salary and benefits package. If successful, you will enjoy NeoDyne's "People First" company culture, built on mutual respect, trust, and honesty, where each individual actively contributes as a valued team member. **What will I be doing?** * Data Centre and Substation Electrical Design: Projects could include preparation of coordinated drawings covering all aspects of installations, including mechanical, electrical, instrumentation and structural elements. More specifically, you will be producing Electrical Design including, Electrical Layouts, LV \& MV Distribution Design, Control \& Instrumentation Design and General Services Design. You will have the opportunity to use state\-of\-the\-art engineering software for control and protection design and substation layouts. * Carrying out Power Systems Electrical Studies: Load Estimates, Protection Coordination, Cable Sizing Calcs. You will have the opportunity to use state\-of\-the\-art engineering software along side leading\-edge power system modelling software to run steady state and transient electrical studies, including short circuit studies, and protection studies for proposed renewable generation * Generating technical documentation: You will be producing specifications for various electrical equipment and assemblies: transformers, RTUs, instrumentation etc. Dealing with customers will also be key parts of our graduate programme to help develop your soft skills. * You will also have the opportunity to travel to customer sites to get involved in installation, testing, and commissioning, by participating in design \& commissioning of SCADA and control systems in the field: I/O and instrumentation testing, full testing and commissioning. **What about training?** *Our induction programme revolves around a 70\-20\-10 model for learning and development.* * 70% On\-the\-job Learning: You'll get stuck in from Day 1, working on real projects, delivering real business value to our customers both on\- and off\-site. * 20% Learning from others: You'll be part of a team, working with subject matter experts and interacting with our customers daily. You'll receive support and guidance, and an invaluable opportunity to learn from former graduates who've seen it and done it. * 10% Formal Education: We'll help you become an expert in your chosen field, backed up by formal e\-learning and classroom training. This extends to attaining professional certifications where relevant to our business specialisations. **Required** **Experience \& skills** * Level 8 Degree in Electrical/ Power Systems/ Energy Engineering, or equivalent. * 0\-2 years' experience in a similar electrical design role or Work Placement. * Full Irish driving license or to be achieved within 12 months of starting the role. Desired * Experience in developing Electrical Schematics using AutoCAD, EPLAN or similar. * Experience in developing equipment layouts using AutoCAD, or similar. **Characteristics** *We look for candidates who* * Work well as part of a team. * Have excellent problem solving and diagnostic skills. * Can complete tasks independently, capable of self\-learning. * Can prioritise and coordinate work. * Have excellent customer service, interpersonal, communication and organisational skills. * Can multitask and work well under pressure. * Are a strategic business thinker. **What's on offer?** * Competitive starting salary with year\-on\-year bonuses and salary increases * Paid Overtime * Paid Graduation Day * Health Insurance * Income Protection * Life Assurance * PRSA pension with employer contribution * Flexible Working * Personalised Career Development Plan * Professional Certifications, pathways to become a Chartered Engineer * Cycle to work scheme **Apply today!** With our diverse range of business specialisations, customers, and industries that we serve, we offer an unrivalled programme designed to accelerate your personal and professional development. Don't miss this opportunity to join a company at the forefront of industrial automation projects in Ireland, the UK, mainland Europe and the US. So why wait? Apply Today! NeoDyne Ltd. is an equal opportunity employer, and all applications will be treated in strictest confidence.

title: Office & Facilities Operations Executive
description: **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About The Office \& Facilities Operations Executive:** The Office \& Facilities Operations Executive is a hands\-on, individual contributor role responsible for the day\-to\-day running of the Dublin office. The role is primarily focused on facilities and workplace operations (approximately 85%), with a smaller element of administrative and executive support (approximately 15%). This role suits someone with prior office or facilities experience who is comfortable working independently, coordinating vendors and stakeholders, and keeping the workplace running smoothly, safely, and professionally. **Role \& Responsibilities:** Facilities \& Workplace Operations (85%) * Manage the day\-to\-day operation of the Dublin office, ensuring a safe, professional, and well\-maintained working environment. * Coordinate cleaning, maintenance, building management systems (BMS), workspace setup, and general office upkeep, including monitoring stock levels of office supplies and consumables. * Act as the main operational point of contact for landlords, building management, and external service providers. * Coordinate vendor activities, manage service providers, track service levels (SLAs), and support facilities\-related procurement. * Track facilities\-related budgets, invoices, and routine operational spend, escalating variances where needed. * Support and coordinate Health \& Safety activities, including maintaining documentation, supporting risk assessments, coordinating incident reporting, and escalating issues in line with company policies and statutory requirements. * Act as a facilities contact during building incidents or emergencies (e.g. fire alarms, building issues), coordinating with building management and internal stakeholders. * Serve as a trained First Aider and Fire Warden (training will be provided) and support ongoing workplace safety and wellbeing initiatives. * Coordinate IT and workplace equipment needs with internal IT teams, including workstation setup and basic asset tracking. * Resolve day\-to\-day facilities or operational issues promptly, escalating more complex matters where appropriate. * Manage visitor access in line with Facilities SOPs (pre\-visit, check\-in, post\-visit follow\-up) and coordinate catering when required. * Work closely with the Information Security team to support physical security controls, including identifying and escalating physical security issues. * Provide regular operational updates to the Facilities Leadership team, covering completed and upcoming works, vendor performance, incidents, and spend. Administrative \& Executive Support (15%) * Support new\-hire onboarding, including workspace setup, access badges, induction coordination, and equipment preparation, in line with internal data protection policies. * Respond to employee queries related to office and facilities matters. * Provide ad\-hoc administrative support to executives, such as meeting coordination, travel bookings, and calendar assistance, where business needs require. * Support office culture and engagement activities, including events and team initiatives. * Maintain facilities and office records, trackers, and documentation to support smooth operations and audit readiness. * Assist with occasional executive schedule changes to accommodate client meetings. **Skills \& Qualifications:** * Previous experience in office management, facilities coordination, or workplace operations. * Proven experience coordinating vendors and day\-to\-day facilities services. * Good working knowledge of workplace Health \& Safety practices in Ireland; experience supporting compliance activities in a regulated environment is desirable. * Strong organisational and time\-management skills, with a practical, hands\-on approach. * Comfortable working independently and managing competing priorities. * Clear communicator with the ability to handle sensitive information discreetly. * Experience supporting onboarding and office logistics. * Fluent in spoken and written English. **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Company Pension Plan * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Education reimbursement * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €40,000 \- €45,000* . *There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* *MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual with a disability and require reasonable accommodation to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* people.success@marketstar.com *for assistance.*

title: PHD Graduate - Data Scientist
description: Purpose of the Position/ Role Summary We are offering an exciting opportunity to work in a rapidly growing global pharmaceutical organisation within the animal health sector. As a PHD Graduate Data Scientist, you will join the Bimeda IT Global Data \& Analytics team, contributing to impactful, data\-driven and AI\-driven initiatives across the business. This 24\-month program is designed to accelerate your development by giving you hands\-on experience with real\-world data and artificial intelligence challenges, exposure to global stakeholders, and the opportunity to deliver meaningful insights and intelligent solutions that support innovation and operational excellence. Role Title: PHD Graduate \- Data Scientist Reporting to: Head of Data Analytics Duration of Post: Fixed Term – 24 Months Contract Type: Full\-time Salary: €62,000\.00 Location: 21 Spruce Avenue, Stillorgan Industrial Park Key Role Specific Requirements * Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights * Develop and deploy predictive models, machine learning, and AI\-driven solutions * Contribute to the design, development, and implementation of AI initiatives across the global Bimeda organisation (e.g., automation, advanced analytics, generative AI use cases) * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand business requirements and translate them into data science and AI solutions * Support the development of data pipelines, AI models, dashboards, and reporting tools * Assist in evaluating and implementing emerging AI technologies and best practices * Contribute to data governance, model governance, and responsible AI practices * Present findings and recommendations to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders * Continuously explore new tools, techniques, and methodologies in data science and artificial intelligence Please note that this job description is not exhaustive, and the role holder may be required to undertake other relevant duties commensurate with the grading of the post. Activities may be subject to amendment over time as the role develops and/or priorities and requirements evolve What We Offer * An exciting opportunity to work in a rapidly growing global pharmaceutical organisation * Access to the latest software and modern data and AI platforms * A chance to work alongside experienced colleagues in data science, AI, IT, and the pharmaceutical industry * Exposure to global projects, cutting\-edge AI initiatives, and diverse datasets * Structured learning and development opportunities in both data science and AI * Mentorship from senior data and AI professionals * A collaborative and innovative working environment Why Join Us? You will be part of a global organisation making a real difference in animal health, while building a strong foundation in both data science and artificial intelligence. This role offers a unique blend of technical challenge, innovation, and real\-world impact **About You** * PHD graduate with a degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Artificial Intelligence, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Experience with programming languages such as Python, R, or similar * Familiarity with data visualisation tools (e.g., Power BI) * Basic understanding of machine learning and AI concepts (e.g., NLP, predictive modelling) * Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts clearly * A proactive mindset and eagerness to learn Desirable Skills * Exposure to AI/ML frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, scikit\-learn) * Experience with large datasets and cloud platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP) * Knowledge of SQL and database management * Familiarity with generative AI tools or concepts (e.g., LLMs, prompt engineering) * Exposure to version control tools (e.g., Git) * Interest in the pharmaceutical or animal health industry

As a **Retail Sales Consultant,** you’ll: * Deliver outstanding customer service. * Work in a target\-driven environment that rewards achievement. * Be part of a team where your input is valued and your success is celebrated. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a busy setting and enjoys sales and working with people. You'll receive full training, so even if you’re new to telecoms, you’ll be set up for success. **Benefits Include** * Earn €14\.15 per hour \+ attractive commission structure! * Continuous support, learning and opportunities for advancement. * Enhanced earning potential through various staff incentives. * Staff offer. * Free eir Wellness Programme \& Employee Assistance Programme. * €500 Employee Referral Scheme. * Discounted healthcare plan. * Pension scheme contribution. * Generous family friendly policies, including fertility, pregnancy loss, menopause and others. * Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion. * Paid leave benefits incl. maternity and sick leave benefits. * Tax saver tickets and bike to work scheme. Expectations From The Role: As a **Retail Sales Consultant,** your key responsibilities will include: * Customer Engagement: Build rapport with customers, understand their needs, and recommend the most suitable mobile and broadband solutions. * Sales Delivery: Consistently meet or exceed individual sales targets while contributing to the store’s overall performance. * Customer Experience: Handle queries and issues efficiently, ensuring every customer leaves satisfied. * Store Operations: Support daily administrative tasks, including stock control, data entry, and cash handling. * Product and Policy Knowledge: Stay up\-to\-date with eir’s product offerings, promotions, and store policies to ensure accurate and compliant service delivery. * Market Awareness: Keep informed of competitor offerings so you can provide customers with the most competitive and relevant information. Flexibility is essential—you’ll be required to work evenings and weekends in line with store trading hours. Requirements For A Successful Application: **Knowledge, Skills And Abilities** * Confidence in engaging with a variety of customers and understanding their needs. * Clear, effective communication skills with a strong ability to explain products and services. * A positive, target\-driven mind\-set with a passion for providing great service. * Interest in mobile phones, broadband, and emerging technologies. **Qualifications** * 1–2 years of experience in retail or sales is desirable, but relevant life experience will also be considered. * A strong customer focus and a proactive attitude are key to success in this role. Others: At eir, we’re all about building connections—whether that’s through our products or our people. Working here means being part of a team that’s passionate about customer experience and driven by results. If you're looking for a role where you can grow your career, develop your leadership skills, and make a real impact—this is the place for you eir reserves the right to conduct appropriate suitability checks in relation to prospective employees including but not limited to reference checking and/or other searches using publicly available information. We are committed to creating an inclusive and supportive work environment. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know, and we will work with you to meet your needs. **\#eirforall.**

title: Parts Advisor
description: Job Description: **What You’ll Do** * Identify and supply the correct parts to trade and retail customers. * Handle all customer inquiries via **phone, email, online**, and **in\-store** with professionalism. * Organise and manage **incoming and outgoing stock** * Update and maintain website. * Ensure orders are **picked, packed, and dispatched** accurately and efficiently. **What You’ll Need** * **No experience needed** – full training will be provided. * Strong **work ethic**, **reliability**, and **presentation**. * A genuine **interest in cars** and the motor trade. * Basic computer literacy. * A **friendly, helpful attitude** and a desire to deliver top\-level customer service. * A clear, confident telephone manner and willingness to learn about our products. **Working Hours** * Monday to Friday: **8:00am – 5:30pm** * Every second Saturday: **8:00am – 3:00pm** **What We Offer** * A **competitive package** * Ongoing **training and development** * Opportunities for **career progression** within a growing company * A positive and supportive **team environment** **Apply Today** To apply, send your **CV to info@omsautoparts.ie** Join OMS Auto Parts and help shape the future of Ford parts supply in Dublin! title: Payroll Analyst - 12 month FTC
description: **Overview** As a **Payroll Administrator (FTC \- 12 month contract)** at Linesight, you will work with highly experienced, culturally diverse, and passionate teams nurturing a strong social dynamic and a shared sense of achievement. You will gain vast industry experience that will open doors for you, extend your skillset and expand your perspective. We will empower, support, and enable you to take real responsibility in your role so that you can take charge of your career progression with us. For us lasting trusted relationships are everything, both with our teams and our clients. You will manage payroll operations for Ireland, the UK, and additional EMEA entities, ensuring accuracy, compliance, and timely delivery. This is a key role within the Global Payroll team, supporting both operational excellence and ongoing payroll transformation initiatives. **The Opportunity** **In this role you will:** * Take full ownership of end\-to\-end payroll processing across assigned countries. * Prepare, review, and submit monthly payroll data inputs to outsourced providers. * Process payroll changes including new hires, terminations, compensation updates, and benefits. * Review and validate payroll drafts, ensuring accuracy before approval and sign\-off. * Manage payroll processing for Ireland (in\-house system) and outsourced payrolls for other regions. * Perform gross\-to\-net validations, including benefits in kind, deductions, and adjustments. * Complete monthly payroll reconciliations and control summaries. * Ensure all payroll payments are processed accurately and on time. * Partner with Finance teams to support month\-end close and respond to payroll\-related queries. * Support audit requests, compliance activities, and payroll reporting. * Deliver a best\- in\-class employee experience through timely, clear, and accurate communication. * Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external payroll providers. * Contribute to ongoing payroll transformation projects, including UK payroll system implementation. **We would love to hear from you if you have:** * Relevant experience in payroll, specifically with Irish and UK payroll legislation and compliance. * Payroll qualifications are not essential, experience is. * Proven experience managing end\-to\-end payroll processes * Experience working with payroll systems (in\-house and/or outsourced environments) * Advanced Excel skills (e.g., pivot tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, large data handling) * Strong understanding of: Gross\-to\-net calculations, Benefits in Kind (BIK) ,Gross\-ups and payroll adjustments. * IPASS (Ireland) or CIPP (UK) is a nice to have, NOT essential * Excellent attention to detail with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills * Confident communicator with experience handling employee queries via email and virtual platforms. * Ability to manage multiple payrolls and deadlines in a fast\-paced environment All interviews are conducted either in person or virtually with video required.

title: Platform Owner - Public Cloud
description: Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Do as a Platform Owner – Public Cloud** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Lead and scale Cloud Engineering \& Operations, partnering with strategic technology vendors to deliver secure, self‑service cloud platforms that accelerate product team delivery and customer value. * Establish robust cloud governance and standards, ensuring excellence across cost management, security operations, deployment practices, compliance, and policy adherence. * Drive operational performance and service quality, defining metrics and controls to improve availability, workload utilisation, SLA performance, service transition rigour, and security posture. * Provide hands‑on technical leadership, setting cloud strategy and architecture direction, resolving complex escalations, and communicating clearly with senior stakeholders. * Build and develop high‑performing engineering teams, including line management, mentoring, talent strategy execution, and fostering accountability through servant leadership. * Support cloud transformation and strategic alignment, ensuring roadmaps, backlogs, and operating models align with business objectives while strengthening senior stakeholder and partner relationships. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * Proven cloud leadership experience (8\+ years) leading Cloud Engineering and/or SRE teams, with a strong engineering background across enterprise‑scale systems, platforms, and operational environments. * Deep expertise in Microsoft Azure, delivering secure, scalable, and fault‑tolerant architectures using PaaS, serverless, containerisation, and Infrastructure‑as‑Code (Terraform preferred), with working knowledge of GCP as an advantage. * Strong operational and financial acumen, including cloud cost management, budgeting, forecasting, workload optimisation, and governance across security operations, compliance, and policy enforcement. * Advanced DevOps, CI/CD and observability capabilities, with hands‑on experience in logging, monitoring, reliability engineering, enterprise‑grade deployments, and advocating modern Agile, Lean, and DevOps practices. * Visionary and forward‑thinking technologist, able to identify emerging technologies, translate them into business value, inspire new ways of working, and successfully lead transformation initiatives. * Exceptional people and partner leadership skills, including managing and influencing third‑party providers, mentoring high‑performing engineering communities, and excelling in stakeholder management, problem solving, and multi‑stream delivery. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-4307

title: Podiatrist - Nursing Home/ Clinic Cover
description: **Who are we looking for?** For us at Spectrum it’s all about building the best team to help people achieve their health goals. This makes for happy patients and happy team members! We’re currently looking for a Full or Part Time Podiatrist to work collaboratively within a multidisciplinary team in our clinics and nursing homes in Dublin and Meath. The FTE salary range for this role is €50,000\-€77,000 per annum, with potential earnings of up to €100,000 with commission. As travel would be required as part of this role a drivers license and access to a car would be preferred. At Spectrum we want to offer you a career, not just a job. We provide clear career progression alongside a transparent salary scale to help our team develop clinically or into leadership roles. We look to cater for those who want to build their clinical skills and those who want to upskill to become a Clinical Lead, Manager or Director. Depending on experience, there is an option to deliver nail surgery! **Some reasons why our team joined us:** * Our comprehensive clinical support structure * Clear career pathways across clinical and leadership roles * Work variety – we provide multiple specialist services and provide training in each * Flexible working – we can cater for Full or Part time contracts and shifts to suit your lifestyle * CPD calendar including both internal and external speakers * CPD bursaries to assist funding external courses * Study leave * Robust systems to help our team provide the best care for their patients * Discounted Health Insurance * Annual leave that increases with length of service * Me Day – an additional day leave to try something new * Monthly Health \& Wellness allowance If this sounds like an opportunity you’re interested in, then take a read through the roles and responsibilities and hit apply below. We would love to hear from you! **What is the role and what are your responsibilities?** * Treat a variety of skin ailments such as verruca’s, corns, calluses, etc. * Assess and treat ingrown toenails, clipping toenails and general feet tidy up * Treat patients with diabetes at increased risk of foot disease who require preventative care and assistance * Determine the condition of a patient’s feet, ankles, or lower legs by reviewing his or her medical history, listening to the patient’s concerns, and performing a physical examination * Diagnose foot, ankle, and lower\-leg problems through physical exams, and other methods * Provide treatment for foot, ankle, and lower leg ailments, such as prescribing special shoe inserts (orthotics) to improve and correct foot and ankle deformities * Give advice and instruction on foot and ankle care and on general wellness techniques * Educate patients on how to improve and/or improve conditions * Liaise with other healthcare professionals, such as GP’s, Physios and other members of multi\-disciplinary teams, to exchange information about the background and progress of patients and Refer patients to other physicians or specialists if they detect larger health problems, such as diabetes * Meet continuous education requirements and maintain an up\-to\-date CPD folder * Write patient case notes and reports and collect statistics; as well as managing their payments, and issue receipts * Place stock orders and log repairs * In some cases, supervise student and junior podiatrists and enhance your teaching skills * Manage clinical risk and have excellent infection control awareness * Be caring, compassionate and professional and always have the patient at the core of everything you do * Help with creating blogs and content for social media and other marketing channels * Work with the marketing team and provide them with on the ground feedback from time to time **What experience and qualifications do I need?** * BSc OR MSc in Podiatry (essential) * CORU registration (essential) * Podiatry Ireland Membership (essential) * Professional Indemnity Insurance (essential) * Private practice experience (preferred) * New grads are welcome to apply **If you have any queries about the role, please contact 01 290 2690** Please note that if you click "apply" you consent to your information to be stored for 12 months for recruitment purposes by Spectrum Health.
