title: Support Pharmacist - Dundrum, Dublin
description: **Support Pharmacist** ====================== **Healthwave, Dundrum, Dublin** **Hours:** 3 or 4 days per week, no late evenings, no Sundays **Optional Saturday rotation:**If you are interested in occasional Saturday shifts, this can be facilitated by joining the Saturday rotation. Shifts are 10am \- 2pm paid at double time, rotation agreed between the pharmacist team **Pay:** €40 to €42 per hour depending on experience and qualifications **Location:** Dundrum Town Centre, Dublin (across from the Balally Luas stop) **Why this role** ----------------- If you're a pharmacist looking for a role that's a bit different from the typical community pharmacy, this is worth a look. Healthwave is Ireland's first digital pharmacy. We dispense to patients nationwide from our Dublin hub, with no walk\-in queues, no late evenings, and no weekend shifts. Patients interact with us by chat and phone, our dispensing is supported by a robotic PillPod system, and we run on Touchstore. Over the last 12 months we've become one of Ireland's largest dispensers of GLP\-1 weight loss treatments, working with leading online doctor partners, which has added cold chain dispensing and significant volume growth to the operation. You'd be joining a team of 20 people (pharmacists, technicians, and patient care colleagues) in a business that's growing quickly and investing in the technology to bring the best possible pharmacy experience to Irish patients. ### **What you'll do** As a support pharmacist, you'll work alongside peer pharmacists and the wider dispensing team to keep prescriptions moving safely and efficiently. Day to day, this includes: * Clinically checking prescriptions across HSE schemes (GMS, DPS, LTI, HTD) and private prescriptions * Dispensing and final\-checking prescriptions for home delivery patients across Ireland * Supporting our cold chain GLP\-1 dispensing operation, one of the fastest\-growing parts of the business * Counselling patients over chat, email and phone using Healthwave's customer support system * Working across our home delivery service and PillPod robotic dispensing system * Liaising with online doctor partners and GPs on prescription queries * Supporting the monthly HSE claim process * Mentoring pharmacy students during their placements We're flexible on exactly how the role develops for the right person. Tell us what areas of pharmacy interest you most. ### **Who we're looking for** * You're a PSI\-registered pharmacist, recent graduates are welcomed * You've worked in an Irish pharmacy setting, whether through pre\-registration training, locum work, or a permanent role * You're comfortable with the HSE schemes and the clinical checking that goes with them * You're confident counselling patients, even when it's by phone or chat rather than face to face * You like working in a team, you're not fazed by technology, and you want to work somewhere that does things a bit differently If you're a new graduate who spent your student placements in a busy dispensary, we'd genuinely like to hear from you. We'll give you the support and mentoring to grow into the role. ### **What we offer** * A growing team of pharmacists in a tech\-forward Irish pharmacy business * 3 or 4 day working week (your choice) * Pharmacy closes 1pm to 2pm for lunch every day * No late evenings, no weekends * €40 to €42 per hour depending on experience and qualifications * Generous in\-store staff discount * Dundrum staff discount scheme * Employee Assistance Programme * Convenient location directly across from the Balally Luas stop **About Healthwave** -------------------- Healthwave launched in 2014 to bring affordable medicines to Irish patients. We've grown to serve over 20,000 members across Ireland from our Dublin dispensing hub. In 2020 we launched PillPods, personalised medication pods that help patients manage complex regimens, which has driven strong growth in our patient base and the team around it. In 2025 we established ourselves as the leading provider of weight loss medications in Ireland. **How to apply** ---------------- Please send your CV with a short cover letter telling us what you're looking for in your next role and why this one interests you. We read every application. *We'll be in touch with candidates we'd like to meet.*

title: Test Engineer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated over 28 years in Technology Services and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver solutions that drive customer success. Our expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly changing Digital\-First world we live in. We foster strong partnerships with leading technology giants including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re An Award\-winning Employer Reflecting How Our Employees Are At The Very Heart Of Version 1 And What We Do * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you! **Job Description** You may have been working as a Software Engineer in Test, Test Engineer or Test Analyst who is confident in their ability performing manual test but also enjoy conducting automation testing. You'll be part of an agile team helping our clients achieve their business transformation goals by identifying requirements, developing solutions and providing ongoing guidance and advice in test. **Key Responsibilities** * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to ensure quality standards are met across projects. * Validate and analyse large volumes of data using SQL. * Develop and maintain reports and dashboards using tools such as SSRS and Power BI. * Support test management processes, leveraging tools like QMetry. * Contribute to automation testing initiatives, with a focus on API testing (experience with Karate is highly desirable). * Participate in knowledge\-sharing activities and foster collaboration across regions and functions. **Qualifications** * Domain Knowledge: Strong understanding of Finance and Insurance comfortable with financial terminology used in business discussions. * Technical Expertise: + Proficiency in SQL for data validation and analysis. + Experience with reporting and data visualisation tools (SSRS, Power BI). * Testing Skills: + Familiarity with test management tools (e.g., QMetry). + Automation testing experience, particularly in API testing (Karate preferred). * Soft Skills: + Clear and proactive communicator. + Strong team player with the ability to work effectively across diverse teams and regions. + Adaptable and eager to learn in a collaborative, inclusive environment. **Work Arrangement** * Office Attendance: This role requires working from the office 4 days per week, Dublin based **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits. * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme. * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance. * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme. * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies. * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat. * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more.

title: The Smooth Company - Internship
description: **The Smooth Company \- Internship Role** **The Role** We are looking for a highly motivated, driven, and enthusiastic intern to join The Smooth Company for a 12\-month placement from June 2026 to June 2027\. This is a unique opportunity to gain hands\-on experience across multiple areas of a fast\-growing, award\-winning beauty brand, working closely with our CEO and the operations and marketing teams. This role will offer exposure to all aspects of the business, from brand and marketing to retail, operations, and content, making it ideal for someone eager to learn, take initiative, and be part of a dynamic, fast\-paced environment. No two days look the same here, and we love it that way. If you're someone who is hard working, thrives on variety, loves a challenge, this could be the perfect fit for you. **What You'll Be Doing** **Events \& Brand Activations** * Travelling across Ireland for pop\-ups, roadshows, and brand activations * Setting up, running, and breaking down event stands and being a face of the brand while you're there * Delivering product demos and engaging warmly with customers * Managing event logistics: kit lists, stock, travel, and on\-site coordination * Taking ownership of a retail space during peak trading periods **Operations \& Office** * Keeping the Smooth Dream House running smoothly, weekly shopping, stocking, and organising the space * Supporting with admin, purchase orders, invoices, and expense tracking * Coordinating with couriers, and logistics partners * Helping with diary management and scheduling for the founder * Researching suppliers and services when new needs come up across the business **Content \& Marketing** * Supporting PR activity press samples, send\-outs, and tracking coverage * Supporting the team on content days filming, capturing behind\-the\-scenes, and creating social\-first content * Helping track influencer mentions, gifting, and seeding activity * Pulling together weekly social performance reports * Keeping an eye on trends, competitors, and the wider beauty world **General Support** * Joining product development conversations and sharing your ideas and perspective * Supporting retailer admin materials, follow\-ups, and pipeline management * Helping track product performance * Jumping in on research projects and whatever else the team needs **Requirements:** * Available for full\-time work from June 2026 to June 2027 * Full Irish driver’s license * Hardworking and happy to get stuck in, whatever the task * Highly organised with the ability to manage multiple projects and responsibilities. * Wonderfully organised you stay ahead, not just keep up * Passion for beauty, business, and marketing * A natural self\-starter who takes ownership without being asked * A great communicator warm, clear, and confident * Positive and energetic, especially when you're out representing the brand * Strong problem\-solving skills and adaptability in a fast\-paced environment. * Proficiency in Google Drive, Microsoft and Canva is desirable, but training will be provided. * A team player who thrives on collaboration but can also work independently. * Comfortable in front of cameras (desirable but not required.) **What We Offer:** * Exposure to all aspects of running a fast\-growing beauty brand * Taking ownership of pop up events * Fun, supportive and ambitious team culture * Smooth Company products allowance **How to Apply** * We'd love to hear from you! Send us your CV and a short note about who you are and why The Smooth Company feels like the right fit. We want to get a real sense of you, so don't be shy.

title: Treasury Analyst (EMEA)
description: SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of enabling human life on Mars. **TREASURY ANALYST (EMEA)** SpaceX is looking for a Treasury Analyst to join our team to ensure that our treasury capabilities meet the increasing needs of our rapidly growing global Starlink business. This position will support various aspects of treasury operations, including new\-country expansions, cash management, FX strategy and execution, fixed\-income investments, payments, and systems. **Responsibilities** * Build and maintain the company’s expanding worldwide banking network * Research and resolve inquiries regarding global cash activities * Support in the execution of foreign exchange trades required to fund SpaceX's global business * Perform risk analyses related to foreign exchange and other risks * Work with banking partners and FX liquidity providers to explore new systems and methodologies for executing trades in restricted currencies * Investigate opportunities to enhance and standardize processes related to FX trading/reporting and fixed income investment trading/reporting * Execute best practices/controls across all treasury functions * Perform reconciliations and build forecasting mechanisms as needed * Work closely with the global treasury team in the US, Singapore and Ireland to design and build risk monitoring frameworks, systems, and reporting dashboards that present risks and mitigation plans or alternatives **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree in finance, accounting, economics, business or other business discipline * 2\+ years of corporate treasury or experience in a multinational corporate treasury function or global bank **Preferred Skills And Experience** * Excellent communication (written and verbal), analytical and interpersonal skills * Ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks under tight deadlines * Comfortable with Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint * Experience with payments/cash transfers and related procedures, controls and systems * Experience with corporate treasury FX trading platforms such as FXAll, 360T, and Bloomberg FXGO * Familiarity with treasury management systems (e.g. Kyriba, SAP, FIS) and online banking platforms **Additional Requirements** * This position is based in Dublin and requires being onsite \- remote work is not considered * Must be available to work extended hours and weekends as needed SpaceX is an Equal Opportunity Employer; employment with SpaceX is governed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications and will not be influenced in any manner by race, color, religion, gender, national origin/ethnicity, veteran status, disability status, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, mental or physical disability or any other legally protected status.

title: Underwriting Analyst (A&H) “Early Career 2026”
description: **Who Are We** American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a leading global insurance organization. Building on 100 years of experience, today AIG member companies provide a wide range of property casualty insurance, life insurance, retirement solutions, and other financial services to customers in more than 80 countries and jurisdictions. These diverse offerings include products and services that help businesses and individuals protect their assets, manage risks and provide for retirement security. **Get to know the business** AIG develops insurance solutions that address the exposures of companies and individuals. We embody the tradition of innovation and bring to market groundbreaking insurance solutions for our rapidly changing world. AIG’s Underwriters embody our tradition of innovation, by bringing to market ground\-breaking insurance solutions for our changing world. AIG’s underwriters help to find insurance solutions for risks from a range of areas including Financial Lines, Accident \& Health, Property, Casualty, Specialty Lines, Cyber and Multinational Clients. **Your start @AIG** Our Early Career Program is aimed at graduates from all disciplines who are looking to integrate a diverse international community and to start a career that nurtures their potential. The Early Career Program is for those who enjoy finding innovative solutions to challenges, who see the value of data and use it on their way to success, and who drive operational excellence. The goal of our program is to develop your talents and interests, so that you can have an exciting and fulfilling career at AIG. In addition to the training on\-the\-job, you will participate in the AIG Academy, an 18th month structured development program designed to support and accelerate your strengths and interests. To help you succeed, we make sure you receive above\-average remuneration, regardless of your degree. You will be accompanied by a mentor, and you will have the chance to participate in global workshops, master classes, as well as networking events to get to know colleagues from AIG worldwide. You will receive a permanent employment contract. **What You Can Expect** * Underwrite new business and manage a portfolio in accordance with the underwriting policy, the authorities entrusted, the strategy, processes and tools of the AIG Group; in compliance with the response times agreed with customers * Monitor the existing portfolio against market trends and propose corrective actions to improve portfolio growth and profitability; * Work in good coordination with all technical, sales, administrative, marketing and claims teams; * Build and maintain good business relationships with broker partners. * Participate, design, lead meetings and technical training with our broker partners and / or customers; * Participate in new product development initiatives **Our ideal candidate** We are looking for a young professional who is eager to learn and passionate about working in a dynamic international environment with the world’s leading companies. You are a team player with an open and positive mindset. You have an interest in data science, customer relations and innovation. Our ideal candidate also has excelled in previous work/internship experience, demonstrated outstanding academic and extracurricular achievement, possesses strong analytical skills and is a team player. You are graduated or soon to be graduated – and preferably within the domain of Business Administration, Economics, Sales Management, Sales Administration, Natural Sciences or a comparable course of study (international degree is an advantage). **Offer** AIG offers a competitive benefit package based on experience and function level accompanied with other advantages like global career opportunities and extensive training and personal development programs. Besides that, we offer you diverse, exciting tasks in a digital and international environment with flexible working hours and smart working. We work in flat hierarchies with real opportunities for creativity. Teamwork is our top priority. **Interested? Become a part of our team!** Send your application with your CV and references, stating your earliest possible start date, via our application portal. **Welcome to a culture of inclusion** We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honoured that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. *AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, colour, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories.* AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to hrdublin@aig.com. At AIG, we value in\-person collaboration as a vital part of our culture, which is why we ask our team members to be primarily in the office. This approach helps us work together effectively and create a supportive, connected environment for our team and clients alike. **Enjoy Benefits That Take Care Of What Matters** At AIG, our people are our greatest asset. We know how important it is to protect and invest in what’s most important to you. That is why we created our Total Rewards Program, a comprehensive benefits package that extends beyond time spent at work to offer benefits focused on your health, wellbeing and financial security—as well as your professional development—to bring peace of mind to you and your family. **Reimagining insurance to make a bigger difference to the world** American International Group, Inc. (AIG) is a global leader in commercial and personal insurance solutions; we are one of the world’s most far\-reaching property casualty networks. It is an exciting time to join us — across our operations, we are thinking in new and innovative ways to deliver ever\-better solutions to our customers. At AIG, you can go further to support individuals, businesses, and communities, helping them to manage risk, respond to times of uncertainty and discover new potential. We invest in our largest asset, our people, through continuous learning and development, in a culture that celebrates everyone for who they are and what they want to become. **Welcome to a culture of inclusion** We’re committed to creating a culture that truly respects and celebrates each other’s talents, backgrounds, cultures, opinions and goals. We foster a culture of inclusion and belonging through learning, cultural awareness activities and Employee Resource Groups (ERGs). With global chapters, ERGs are a cornerstone for our culture of inclusion. The talent of our people is one of AIG’s greatest assets, and we are honored that our drive for positive change has been recognized by numerous recent awards and accreditations. *AIG provides equal opportunity to all qualified individuals regardless of race, color, religion, age, gender, gender expression, national origin, veteran status, disability or any other legally protected categories.* AIG is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to job applicants and employees with disabilities. If you believe you need a reasonable accommodation, please send an email to candidatecare@aig.com. **Functional Area** UW \- Underwriting AIG Europe S.A. (Ireland branch)

title: Zambrero Sales and Catering assistant - Part-time
description: We are seeking an **energetic, positive, and customer\-focused individual** to join our team at **M3 Mulhuddart**. This is a Part time position. #### **Employee Benefits** * **60% Staff Discount** * **Peer Awards Scheme** * **Employee Referral Scheme** * **Free On\-site Staff Parking** * **Excellent Career Progression Opportunities** * **Bike to Work Scheme** * **Premium Pay for Sunday Work** * **Premium Pay for Night Work** #### **Key Responsibilities** * **Food Preparation:** Cooking, serving, slicing, and chopping \- all food\-related duties within the kitchen. * **Customer Experience:** Ensuring every customer leaves with a smile and a great experience. * **Product Knowledge:** Understanding the company's offerings so you can confidently answer customer questions. * **Organisation:** Assisting wherever needed to maintain smooth operations. * **Teamwork:** Contributing positively to the team environment and supporting your colleagues. * **Cleaning \& Hygiene:** Maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness and hygiene in all areas. #### **The Ideal Candidate Will Have** * **Customer Focus:** A passion for providing excellent service and understanding that the customer always comes first. * **Problem Solving:** The ability to address and resolve customer concerns efficiently. * **HACCP Knowledge:** Familiarity with food hygiene systems (desirable). * **Personal Drive:** A proactive attitude \- you take initiative and get things done. * **Attention to Detail:** Ensuring all tasks are completed to a high standard. * **Integrity:** Honesty, reliability, and respect for colleagues and customers alike. * **Team Spirit:** A strong team player who knows when to lead and when to support. * **Food Knowledge:** Desirable but not essential \- we'll provide training! **Please Note:** At this time, we do **not** require the assistance of recruitment agencies.
